title: Spicy Honey Noodles
description: Sweet heat, slick noodles, garlic in the mix, and just enough attitude to keep dinner from being boring.
instructions: Boil the noodles according to the package instructions, then drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the honey, soy sauce, chili crisp, garlic, and rice vinegar.
Heat the butter or sesame oil in a pan over medium heat and pour in the sauce mixture.
Let it bubble for 1 to 2 minutes until glossy and fragrant.
Add the noodles and toss until everything is evenly coated.
Plate the noodles and finish with scallions and sesame seeds.
notes: Add shrimp, crispy tofu, or shredded chicken if you want more protein.
Use less chili crisp if you want the heat toned down.
A squeeze of lime at the end wakes the whole thing up.
whyThisHits: This one works because it lands in that sweet spot between lazy and impressive. It is fast enough for a weeknight, but still feels like you meant business.
pairing: Try it with a cold lager, sparkling water with lime, or a crunchy cucumber salad if you want to cool the fire down a little.
footerLine: Art for Your Mouth. Because boring food is a waste of time.
